articles.title: Heatwave alert for eight districts in Himachal today - The Tribune
articles.search_term: heatwave (Bilaspur OR Chamba OR Hamirpur OR Kangra OR Kinnaur OR Kullu OR "Lahaul and Spiti" OR Mandi OR Shimla OR "Sirmaur (Sirmour)" OR Solan OR Una) Himachal Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Unlock Exclusive Insights with The Tribune Premium
Take your experience further with Premium access. Thought-provoking Opinions, Expert Analysis, In-depth Insights and other Member Only BenefitsHimachal Pradesh is likely to experience extensive heatwave conditions on May 27, with the Meteorological Centre issuing a yellow weather warning for eight districts of the state. The alert has been issued for Shimla, Solan, Mandi, Kangra, Una, Bilaspur, Hamirpur and Sirmaur districts, where heatwave conditions are expected at isolated places.
The weather office has also sounded a similar yellow alert for several districts on May 28. According to the department, both maximum and minimum temperatures across the state are likely to rise by 2°C to 5°C over the next two days. However, a significant change in weather is expected from May 28 onwards, when many parts of the state are likely to witness heavy rain and hailstorm activity, leading to a fall in temperatures by 2°C to 6°C.
During the past 24 hours, the weather remained mostly dry across the state, though a few places recorded very light rainfall. Minimum temperatures remained close to normal and ranged between 3°C and 25°C, while maximum temperatures stayed 2°C to 4°C above normal, ranging between 14°C and 42°C.
Una was the hottest place in the state with a maximum temperature of 42.6°C.

articles.title: IMD Issues Heatwave, Rain Alert For Himachal Pradesh - Dailyhunt
articles.full_text:
The IMD has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions in several Himachal Pradesh districts before forecasting rain, snowfall, thunderstorms
May 26, 2026: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a dual weather alert for Himachal Pradesh, warning of severe heatwave conditions over the next two days followed by widespread rain, snowfall and hailstorms later this week. According to the Meteorological Centre in Shimla, dry weather is expected on May 26 and 27, leading to a sharp rise in temperatures across several districts. A yellow alert has been issued for isolated areas of Una, Bilaspur, Hamirpur, Kangra, Solan, Shimla and Sirmaur.
Officials said both minimum and maximum temperatures could rise by 2 to 5 degrees Celsius during the ongoing heatwave phase. Una recorded the highest temperature in the state at 41.6 degrees Celsius, while Keylong remained the coldest with a minimum temperature of 2.5 degrees Celsius. However, weather conditions are expected to change from May 28 as a fresh Western Disturbance approaches northwest India, bringing light to moderate rainfall and snowfall across many parts of the state.
The IMD has also forecast thunderstorms, lightning, hailstorms and gusty winds reaching up to 60 kmph in some regions, especially in high-altitude districts such as Kinnaur and Lahaul-Spiti. Authorities have advised residents to avoid heat exposure, stay hydrated and remain indoors during thunderstorms. Farmers have been urged to protect crops and orchards from hail and strong winds through precautionary measures. The weather department said temperatures are expected to fall significantly after the wet spell, bringing relief from the prevailing heat conditions.
